visual_notes: The preserved wood grain and annual rings are the defining visual feature — the stone tells a biological story. Cross-sections are among the most visually striking mineral-art objects, showing perfect tree anatomy in glowing chalcedony. Color zonation often follows the original wood structure.
prompt_fragments: petrified wood cross-section showing ancient tree rings in chalcedony
rust-red Triassic log silicified in Arizona desert
deep time mineral wood grain preserved in stone
vibe_notes: Petrified wood is the most legible deep-time object — a tree that became a stone, grain by grain, over millions of years, while preserving every ring and vessel. It is the bridge between biology and mineralogy, and one of the most emotionally resonant materials in the natural world.
folklore_summary: Many Indigenous traditions of the American Southwest regard petrified wood as sacred — the bones of ancient beings or the material remains of primordial forests.
